metadata.validation: SRS-aware with placeholder blocking and deduplication

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application from App Store
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that a user can successfully download the mobile application from the App Store.
preconditions: User has an iOS device with sufficient storage space
test_steps: Step 1: Open the App Store on the mobile device and search for 'Restaurant/Food Service' app
Step 2: Select the app icon and click the 'Get' button to initiate download
Step 3: Wait for the download to complete, then tap the 'Install' button to install the app
Step 4: Verify that the app is successfully installed on the mobile device
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application is successfully downloaded and installed on the iOS device, with no errors or warnings.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid App Store search query
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that an invalid search query returns an error message.
preconditions: User has an iOS device with sufficient storage space
test_steps: Step 1: Open the App Store on the mobile device and enter an invalid search query (e.g., 'abc123')
Step 2: Tap the 'Search' button to initiate the search
Step 3: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that no results were found
Step 4: Confirm that the app does not download or install
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed indicating that no results were found, and the app does not download or install.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Maximum number of search results
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the maximum number of search results is displayed.
preconditions: User has an iOS device with sufficient storage space
test_steps: Step 1: Open the App Store on the mobile device and enter a valid search query (e.g., 'pizza')
Step 2: Tap the 'Search' button to initiate the search
Step 3: Verify that the maximum number of search results (100) is displayed
Step 4: Confirm that no more results are returned when scrolling
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The maximum number of search results (100) is displayed, and no more results are returned when scrolling.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Integration with other features
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application integrates correctly with other features.
preconditions: User has an iOS device with sufficient storage space
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application on the iOS device
Step 2: Navigate to the search page and enter a valid search query (e.g., 'pizza')
Step 3: Tap the 'Search' button to initiate the search
Step 4: Verify that the search results are displayed correctly, including any relevant information
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application integrates correctly with other features, displaying search results as expected.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting information link for a restaurant
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that selecting the information link directs to an information page with expected details
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile application and select 'Restaurant Information' link
Step 2: Verify that the link redirects to a new page with restaurant name, address, phone number, email, type of food, average price, and menu details
Step 3: Confirm that the page displays a picture of the restaurant and a menu with dish names, descriptions, and prices
Step 4: Verify that the page does not display any unnecessary or irrelevant information
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The user is redirected to an information page displaying expected details about the restaurant, including name, address, phone number, email, type of food, average price, and menu with dish names, descriptions, and prices

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid restaurant information link
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that an invalid restaurant information link returns an error message or redirects to a default page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile application and select 'Restaurant Information' link with an invalid ID (e.g., 9999)
Step 2: Verify that the link does not redirect to a valid information page
Step 3: Confirm that the page displays an error message indicating that the restaurant information is not available or redirects to a default page
Step 4: Verify that the page does not display any unnecessary or irrelevant information
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The user is presented with an error message or redirected to a default page when attempting to access invalid restaurant information link

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Maximum number of menu items displayed
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the maximum number of menu items are displayed when selecting a restaurant information link
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile application and select 'Restaurant Information' link for a specific restaurant
Step 2: Verify that the page displays the maximum number of menu items (e.g., 10)
Step 3: Confirm that the page does not display more than the maximum number of menu items
Step 4: Verify that the page does not display any unnecessary or irrelevant information
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The user is presented with a maximum of 10 menu items when selecting a restaurant information link, without displaying any unnecessary or irrelevant information

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Restaurant information link integration with search results
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that selecting a restaurant information link from search results redirects to the correct information page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile application and perform a search for a specific restaurant
Step 2: Verify that the search results include the selected restaurant's information link
Step 3: Click on the information link from the search results page
Step 4: Confirm that the user is redirected to the correct information page with expected details
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The user is successfully redirected to the correct restaurant information page when selecting the information link from search results, displaying expected details about the restaurant

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application successfully
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application can be downloaded without any issues.
preconditions: The user has a compatible device and internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Google Play Store at https://play.google.com/store
Step 2: Search for 'Restaurant Finder' in the search bar
Step 3: Click on the 'Install' button to begin the download process
Step 4: Verify that the application is successfully installed and launched
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application is successfully downloaded and installed, with no errors or warnings.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Error handling for invalid download attempt
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the application handles an invalid download attempt correctly.
preconditions: The user has a compatible device and internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Google Play Store at https://play.google.com/store
Step 2: Search for 'Invalid App Name' in the search bar
Step 3: Click on the 'Install' button to attempt to download the non-existent application
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that the application cannot be found
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed indicating that the application cannot be found, with no attempts to download or install the application.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application at edge case
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the application can be downloaded successfully at the edge case.
preconditions: The user has a compatible device and internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Google Play Store at https://play.google.com/store
Step 2: Search for 'Restaurant Finder' in the search bar
Step 3: Click on the 'Install' button to begin the download process while the device is at its maximum storage capacity
Step 4: Verify that the application is successfully installed and launched, despite the edge case
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application is successfully downloaded and installed, with no errors or warnings, even when the device is at its maximum storage capacity.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Integration test for download mobile application
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the download process integrates correctly with other features.
preconditions: The user has a compatible device and internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Google Play Store at https://play.google.com/store
Step 2: Search for 'Restaurant Finder' in the search bar
Step 3: Click on the 'Install' button to begin the download process while logged into a Google account
Step 4: Verify that the application is successfully installed and launched, with the user's login credentials retained
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application is successfully downloaded and installed, with no errors or warnings, and the user's login credentials are retained correctly.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Switch to map view for search results
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate the ability to switch between map and list views for all search options
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Italian' in cuisine field: 'Italian'
Step 3: Click 'Search' button with no filters applied
Step 4: Verify map view displays all search results within a 5km radius, sorted by distance first and then by rating
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Map view correctly displays all search results within a 5km radius, sorted by distance first and then by rating, with exact count of 10-20 restaurants

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid search query for map view
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate error handling when searching with an invalid query
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'invalid' in cuisine field: 'invalid'
Step 3: Click 'Search' button with no filters applied
Step 4: Verify error message 'Invalid query. Please try again.' is displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message 'Invalid query. Please try again.' is correctly displayed, and the map view remains unchanged

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Maximum search results for map view
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate the maximum number of search results displayed in map view
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Italian' in cuisine field: 'Italian'
Step 3: Click 'Search' button with no filters applied and maximum results set to 100
Step 4: Verify map view displays exactly 100 search results within a 5km radius, sorted by distance first and then by rating
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Map view correctly displays exactly 100 search results within a 5km radius, sorted by distance first and then by rating, with no more or fewer results displayed

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search result filtering for map view
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate the integration of search filters with map view
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Italian' in cuisine field: 'Italian'
Step 3: Click 'Filter by distance' and select 5km radius
Step 4: Verify map view displays only restaurants within the selected 5km radius, sorted by distance first and then by rating
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Map view correctly filters search results to display only restaurants within the selected 5km radius, sorted by distance first and then by rating, with exact count of 10-20 restaurants

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by price with invalid range
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate error handling for an invalid price range
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '-10' and maximum price '500' in the price range fields
Step 3: Click 'Search' button with invalid price range -10-500
Step 4: Verify error message is displayed indicating an invalid price range
Step 5: Confirm search results are not displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives an error message indicating an invalid price range of -$10 to $500, and no search results are displayed

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid input for search by destination
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate error handling when invalid inputs are provided for the distance range
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum distance '-5' and maximum distance '10' in the distance fields
Step 3: Click 'Search' button with no filters applied
Step 4: Verify an error message is displayed indicating invalid input for minimum distance
Step 5: Confirm search results are not displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message 'Minimum distance must be a positive value' is displayed, and no search results are shown

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination with invalid minimum distance
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate error handling when inputting an invalid minimum distance
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to Search page at /search
Step 2: Input destination 'New York' in the search field
Step 3: Enter minimum distance '-10' and maximum distance '50' in the distance range fields
Step 4: Verify an error message is displayed indicating invalid minimum distance
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message 'Minimum distance cannot be negative' is displayed, and the search results are not updated

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid Price Input for Search
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that an invalid integer input for price search is rejected and displays an error message
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'price' as the search criteria and select 'abc-500' as the range (invalid input)
Step 3: Click 'Search' button with invalid price range 'abc-500'
Step 4: Verify an error message is displayed indicating that the input is not a valid integer
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives an error message when attempting to search with invalid price range 'abc-500', indicating that the input is not a valid integer

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Price Search with Destination Filter
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the price search feature integrates correctly with the destination filter
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'price' as the search criteria and select '100-500' as the range
Step 3: Select a specific destination (e.g., 'New York') from the filter options
Step 4: Click 'Search' button with price range 100-500 and destination filter applied
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully searches for restaurants within the specified price range (100-500) and location ('New York') and displays at least 10 results

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid price and destination search with error handling
phase1_test_cases.description: Test that the system handles invalid input for price and destination search
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to /search/price-destination page
Step 2: Enter invalid price range '-100-500' in price field
Step 3: Select invalid destination 'Unknown City' from dropdown list
Step 4: Click 'Search' button and verify error message is displayed
Step 5: Confirm that the user is prompted to enter valid input
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives an error message indicating invalid input and is prompted to enter valid price range and destination for search results

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Price and destination search integration with restaurant details page
phase1_test_cases.description: Test that the system integrates price and destination search with restaurant details page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to /search/price-destination page
Step 2: Enter valid price range '100-500' in price field
Step 3: Select valid destination 'New York City' from dropdown list
Step 4: Click 'Search' button and select a restaurant from the results
Step 5: Verify that the restaurant details page is displayed with correct information
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully searches for restaurants within the specified price range and distance, selects a restaurant from the results, and is redirected to the correct restaurant details page with accurate information

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by restaurant type: Italian
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate search functionality for Italian restaurants
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page at /search
Step 2: Select 'Italian' from the restaurant type dropdown list
Step 3: Click the 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that only Italian restaurants are displayed in the results
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search results display a maximum of 100 Italian restaurants, sorted by distance first and then by price, with exact addresses and prices

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid restaurant type: Chinese
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate error handling for invalid restaurant types
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page at /search
Step 2: Select 'Chinese' from the restaurant type dropdown list (invalid option)
Step 3: Click the 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating an invalid restaurant type
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed stating 'Invalid restaurant type. Please select a valid option.' and the search results are not displayed

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by restaurant type: All types selected
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate search functionality for all restaurant types selected
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page at /search
Step 2: Select 'All' from the restaurant type dropdown list (selecting all options)
Step 3: Click the 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that all restaurants are displayed in the results, sorted by distance first and then by price
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search results display a maximum of 100 restaurants from all types, sorted by distance first and then by price, with exact addresses and prices

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by restaurant type: Integration with dashboard
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate search functionality integration with the dashboard
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the dashboard at /dashboard
Step 2: Click the 'Search' button on the dashboard
Step 3: Select a restaurant type from the dropdown list (e.g. Italian)
Step 4: Verify that the search results are displayed on the dashboard, sorted by distance first and then by price
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search results are displayed on the dashboard, with a maximum of 100 Italian restaurants, sorted by distance first and then by price, with exact addresses and prices

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for specific dish by name
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate search functionality with a valid dish name
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Chicken Fajita' in the search field
Step 3: Click the 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify the search results contain at least one dish with name 'Chicken Fajita'
Step 5: Confirm the search results are sorted by distance first, then by price
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search results display a list of dishes containing 'Chicken Fajita' with correct sorting and filtering

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid search query
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate error handling for invalid search queries
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'invalid-search-query' in the search field
Step 3: Click the 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify an error message is displayed indicating invalid query
Step 5: Confirm the search results are empty
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message and returns no search results for invalid queries

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with exact match
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate search functionality with an exact match
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Chicken Fajita' (exact match) in the search field
Step 3: Click the 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify the search results contain only one dish with name 'Chicken Fajita'
Step 5: Confirm the search results are sorted by distance first, then by price
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system returns a single result for exact matches

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search integration with filters
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate search functionality with price and distance filters
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Chicken Fajita' in the search field
Step 3: Select a price range of $100-$500 and distance of 5 km
Step 4: Click the 'Search' button
Step 5: Verify the search results contain at least one dish with name 'Chicken Fajita' within the selected price and distance ranges
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system returns search results filtered by price and distance for a specific dish

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid search query
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate error handling when searching with an invalid query
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile application's search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'invalid query' in the free-text search field
Step 3: Click 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify an error message is displayed indicating that no results were found
Step 5: Confirm that the error message does not contain any sensitive information
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives an error message when searching with an invalid query, and the error message is informative but does not reveal sensitive information

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with empty query
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate search functionality when searching with an empty query
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile application's search page at /search
Step 2: Leave the free-text search field blank
Step 3: Click 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that no results are displayed or an error message is shown indicating that no query was provided
Step 5: Confirm that the user can still navigate to other features without issues
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives a clear indication when searching with an empty query, and the search functionality does not crash or display incorrect results

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search integration with restaurant types
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate search functionality when searching by restaurant type
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile application's search page at /search
Step 2: Select 'Italian' from the restaurant type dropdown menu
Step 3: Click 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that results are displayed with a minimum of 5 Italian restaurants matching the search query
Step 5: Confirm that the first result is indeed an Italian restaurant
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully searches for Italian restaurants and receives a list of at least 5 matching results with their names, addresses, and descriptions

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants with no match found
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that when searching for restaurants and no match is found, the user is informed but kept on the search page.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search/restaurants
Step 2: Enter 'Italian' in cuisine field
Step 3: Click 'Search' button with no match found
Step 4: Verify error message 'No matching restaurants found' is displayed
Step 5: Confirm search page URL remains the same
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message 'No matching restaurants found' is displayed, and user remains on search page with no redirect.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid cuisine input for no match found
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that when searching for restaurants with invalid cuisine input and no match is found, the user is informed but kept on the search page.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search/restaurants
Step 2: Enter 'InvalidCuisine' in cuisine field
Step 3: Click 'Search' button with no match found
Step 4: Verify error message 'No matching restaurants found' is displayed
Step 5: Confirm search page URL remains the same
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message 'No matching restaurants found' is displayed, and user remains on search page with no redirect.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants with exact match found
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that when searching for restaurants and an exact match is found, the user is redirected to the restaurant details page.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search/restaurants
Step 2: Enter 'Italian' in cuisine field
Step 3: Click 'Search' button with exact match found
Step 4: Verify redirect to restaurant details page at /restaurant/<exact_match_id>
Step 5: Confirm restaurant details page displays correct information
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to restaurant details page with exact match found, displaying correct information.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants with no match found and invalid input
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that when searching for restaurants with no match found and invalid input, the user is informed but kept on the search page.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search/restaurants
Step 2: Enter 'InvalidCuisine' in cuisine field
Step 3: Click 'Search' button with no match found and invalid input
Step 4: Verify error message 'No matching restaurants found' is displayed
Step 5: Confirm search page URL remains the same
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message 'No matching restaurants found' is displayed, and user remains on search page with no redirect.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid sorting option
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate error handling when selecting an invalid sorting option
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant list page at /restaurants
Step 2: Click 'Sort' button with option 'InvalidOption'
Step 3: Verify error message 'Invalid sorting option' is displayed
Step 4: Confirm the results are not sorted
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message 'Invalid sorting option' is displayed, and the results are not sorted

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid sorting option: error handling
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that an invalid sorting option returns an error message
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'invalid_sorting_option' in search field: 'Invalid Sort'
Step 3: Click 'Search' button with sorting by restaurant name selected
Step 4: Verify error message is displayed indicating invalid sorting option
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message 'Invalid sorting option. Please select a valid option.' is displayed, and no results are returned

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sorting by restaurant name: edge case - single result
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that sorting results by restaurant name displays a single matching restaurant when only one result is found
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'single_result_restaurant' in search field: 'Single Result'
Step 3: Click 'Search' button with sorting by restaurant name selected
Step 4: Verify a single matching result is displayed, sorted alphabetically by restaurant name
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: A single matching result is displayed, sorted alphabetically by restaurant name, with exact expected value 'Single Result'

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sorting by restaurant name: integration with search results
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that sorting results by restaurant name integrates correctly with search results
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'multiple_results_restaurants' in search field: 'Multiple Results'
Step 3: Click 'Search' button with sorting by restaurant name selected
Step 4: Verify search results are sorted alphabetically by restaurant name, displaying multiple matching restaurants
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Search results are displayed in alphabetical order by restaurant name, with a minimum of 5 matching restaurants

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid price range input
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that an invalid price range input returns an error message
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Italian' in cuisine field and select 'Pasta'
Step 3: Click 'Search' button with price range '-10-50'
Step 4: Verify error message 'Invalid price range' is displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message 'Invalid price range' is displayed, indicating that the input is invalid and no results are returned

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sorting by distance: Closets to furthest distance
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that results are sorted by distance from user's position
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page with URL '/search'
Step 2: Enter 'Pizza' in the search field and press Enter
Step 3: Click 'Sort by distance' button
Step 4: Verify that results are sorted by distance from user's position, displaying maximum 100 restaurants
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Results are sorted by distance from user's position, displaying maximum 100 restaurants in the correct order

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid search query: No results returned
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that no results are returned when an invalid search query is entered
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page with URL '/search'
Step 2: Enter 'InvalidSearchQuery' in the search field and press Enter
Step 3: Click 'Sort by distance' button
Step 4: Verify that no results are returned
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: No results are returned when an invalid search query is entered, with a clear error message indicating the issue

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sorting by distance: Edge case - Exactly 100 results
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that exactly 100 results are returned when sorting by distance
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page with URL '/search'
Step 2: Enter 'Pizza' in the search field and press Enter
Step 3: Click 'Sort by distance' button
Step 4: Verify that exactly 100 results are returned
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Exactly 100 results are returned when sorting by distance, with the correct order and no duplicates

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sorting by distance: Integration with search results
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that sorting by distance works correctly when searching for multiple types of restaurants
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page with URL '/search'
Step 2: Enter 'Pizza Italian' in the search field and press Enter
Step 3: Click 'Sort by distance' button
Step 4: Verify that results are sorted by distance from user's position, displaying maximum 100 restaurants
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Sorting by distance works correctly when searching for multiple types of restaurants, with the correct order and no duplicates

phase1_test_cases.test_title: New release notification for restaurant owners
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that restaurant owners receive notifications when a new or updated version of the software is released
test_steps: Step 1: Log in to restaurant owner dashboard at /restaurant-owner/dashboard
Step 2: Navigate to 'Notifications' page and verify no new releases are listed
Step 3: Simulate a new release by updating the software version in the database
Step 4: Verify that the 'Notifications' page now lists the new release with details (version number, date released)
Step 5: Confirm that the notification includes a link to the release notes and a download button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Restaurant owner successfully receives notification of new software release with version number v2.1.0 and link to release notes, allowing for easy download and installation

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid new release notification for restaurant owners
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that restaurant owners do not receive notifications when an invalid or non-existent software version is released
test_steps: Step 1: Log in to restaurant owner dashboard at /restaurant-owner/dashboard
Step 2: Navigate to 'Notifications' page and verify no new releases are listed
Step 3: Simulate an invalid software release by updating the database with a non-existent version number (e.g. v999.0.0)
Step 4: Verify that the 'Notifications' page does not list the invalid release
Step 5: Confirm that no notification is sent to the restaurant owner
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Restaurant owner does not receive notification of non-existent software release v999.0.0, and the 'Notifications' page remains unchanged

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Edge case: No new releases for restaurant owners
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that restaurant owners do not receive notifications when there are no new or updated software versions available
test_steps: Step 1: Log in to restaurant owner dashboard at /restaurant-owner/dashboard
Step 2: Navigate to 'Notifications' page and verify no new releases are listed
Step 3: Verify that the database does not contain any new or updated software versions
Step 4: Confirm that the 'Notifications' page remains empty
Step 5: Verify that no notification is sent to the restaurant owner
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Restaurant owner does not receive any notifications, and the 'Notifications' page remains empty since there are no new or updated software versions available

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Integration with restaurant owner dashboard

Step 4: Verify that the 'Notifications' page now lists the new release with details (version number, date released)
Step 5: Confirm that the notification is displayed correctly on the restaurant owner dashboard
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: New release notification is displayed correctly on the restaurant owner dashboard, including version number v2.1.0 and date released, allowing for easy tracking of software updates

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download and notify users of new releases - Positive Test Case
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that users receive notifications for new releases and can download them successfully.
preconditions: User has a mobile phone with internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant's mobile app store page at https://example.com/mobile-app-store
Step 2: Search for the restaurant's mobile application in the app store and select it
Step 3: Click the 'Update' button to download the new release
Step 4: Verify that the download is successful and the user receives a notification about the new release
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully downloads the new release and receives a notification with the correct version number and release date.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download and notify users of new releases - Negative Test Case

Step 2: Search for an invalid application in the app store and select it
Step 3: Click the 'Update' button to download the new release (expected error)
Step 4: Verify that the system displays an error message indicating that the application is not found
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: System displays an error message indicating that the application is not found, and no download attempt is made.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download and notify users of new releases - Edge Test Case
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system handles edge cases correctly.

Step 2: Search for the restaurant's mobile application in the app store and select it (exact match)
Step 3: Click the 'Update' button to download the new release (edge case: exact match)
Step 4: Verify that the system displays a notification about the new release with the correct version number and release date
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: System displays a notification about the new release with the correct version number and release date, and the download is successful.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download and notify users of new releases - Integration Test Case

phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives a notification about the new release, and the download is successful, allowing access to the updated features.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid price range filter

phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message indicating invalid input for price range, and restores the original search results.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User views profile page with valid data

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Profile page displays maximum 100 results

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User can set language on Profile page

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Language setting persists across sessions

phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Selected language is persisted across different user sessions, displaying French labels and text on the Profile page.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User registration with valid credentials
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate user registration with correct username and password

Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123' in password field
Step 4: Click 'Register' button and verify successful registration message
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully registered with username john.doe@example.com and password SecurePass123, displaying confirmation message 'Registration successful'

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid username during registration
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate error handling for invalid usernames during user registration
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile app login page at /login
Step 2: Enter username 'invalid_username' in email field
Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123' in password field
Step 4: Click 'Register' button and verify error message for invalid username
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message 'Invalid username. Please try again' is displayed, preventing registration with invalid username

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Registration with empty password
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate error handling for empty passwords during user registration
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile app login page at /login
Step 2: Enter username 'john.doe@example.com' in email field
Step 3: Leave password field empty
Step 4: Click 'Register' button and verify error message for missing password
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message 'Password is required. Please try again' is displayed, preventing registration with empty password

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Registration integration with existing user

Step 2: Enter username 'john.doe@example.com' in email field (already registered)
Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123' in password field
Step 4: Click 'Register' button and verify error message for existing user
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message 'User already exists. Please log in instead' is displayed, preventing duplicate registration

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User registration with valid inputs

test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile app's registration page at /register
Step 2: Enter username 'johnDoe' in username field
Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123' in password field
Step 4: Enter email address 'johndoe@example.com' in email field
Step 5: Click 'Register' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully registered with username johnDoe, password SecurePass123, and email address johndoe@example.com.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User registration with invalid password
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate user registration fails when provided password is too short.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile app's registration page at /register
Step 2: Enter username 'johnDoe' in username field
Step 3: Enter password 'short' in password field (less than 8 characters)
Step 4: Enter email address 'johndoe@example.com' in email field
Step 5: Click 'Register' button and verify error message
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message 'Password must be at least 8 characters long' is displayed, and registration fails.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User registration with maximum length username
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate user registration allows usernames up to the maximum allowed length.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile app's registration page at /register
Step 2: Enter username 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz' in username field (maximum allowed length)
Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123' in password field
Step 4: Enter email address 'johndoe@example.com' in email field
Step 5: Click 'Register' button and verify successful registration
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully registered with username abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz, password SecurePass123, and email address johndoe@example.com.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User registration with existing username
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate user registration fails when provided username already exists.
preconditions: Mobile application is installed, and a test user has been registered
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile app's registration page at /register
Step 2: Enter username 'johnDoe' in username field (already taken)
Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123' in password field
Step 4: Enter email address 'johndoe@example.com' in email field
Step 5: Click 'Register' button and verify error message
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message 'Username already taken, please choose a different one' is displayed, and registration fails.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User can choose to provide a regularly used phone number during registration
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the user can select their phone number as an option during registration and it is stored correctly in the system.
preconditions: The user has not registered before
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile application registration page at /register
Step 2: Enter username 'johnDoe' in username field, password 'SecurePass123' in password field, and phone number '+1-555-1234' in phone number field
Step 3: Select the option to provide a regularly used phone number
Step 4: Click 'Register' button
Step 5: Verify that the user's profile is created with the provided phone number
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The user's profile is successfully created and their regularly used phone number is stored correctly in the system.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid phone number input during registration results in error message
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the system displays an error message when an invalid phone number is entered during registration.
preconditions: The user has not registered before
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile application registration page at /register
Step 2: Enter username 'johnDoe' in username field, password 'SecurePass123' in password field, and phone number '+123-4567' in phone number field (invalid format)
Step 3: Click 'Register' button
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating the invalid phone number format
Step 5: Confirm that the user's profile is not created
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message indicating the invalid phone number format and does not create a user profile.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Maximum length of regularly used phone number during registration
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the system allows a maximum length of [specific length] for the regularly used phone number during registration.
preconditions: The user has not registered before
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile application registration page at /register
Step 2: Enter username 'johnDoe' in username field, password 'SecurePass123' in password field, and phone number '+1-555-12345678' in phone number field (maximum length)
Step 3: Click 'Register' button
Step 4: Verify that the user's profile is created with the maximum length regularly used phone number
Step 5: Confirm that the system does not display an error message
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system allows a maximum length of [specific length] for the regularly used phone number during registration and creates a user profile with the provided phone number.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User registration integrates with existing user profiles
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the system correctly integrates new user registrations with existing user profiles.
preconditions: The user has not registered before
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to mobile application login page at /login
Step 2: Enter username 'existingUser' in username field and password 'SecurePass123' in password field (existing user)
Step 3: Click 'Login' button
Step 4: Navigate to mobile application registration page at /register
Step 5: Enter username 'newUser' in username field, password 'SecurePass123' in password field, and phone number '+1-555-1234' in phone number field (new user)
Step 6: Click 'Register' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system correctly integrates new user registrations with existing user profiles, allowing the new user to log in and access their profile.

Step 5: Confirm that the application does not crash or freeze during the integration operation
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays a list of restaurants and integrates correctly with the restaurant data without exceeding maximum memory usage (10 MB) and does not crash or freeze during the integration operation.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid search criteria

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search integration with dashboard

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Application Portability: iOS and Android Compatibility
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the application is portable across iOS and Android platforms.
preconditions: The application is installed on both an iOS device and an Android device

Step 3: Verify that the restaurant list is displayed correctly, including distance and price information
Step 4: Switch to an Android device and repeat steps 1-3 to validate compatibility
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays the same restaurant list on both iOS and Android devices, with correct distance and price information.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid Platform: Application Fails to Launch

Step 2: Verify that the application does not launch and displays an error message

Step 4: Confirm that the application is not installed or launched on the Windows device
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application fails to launch on the Windows device, displaying an error message indicating platform incompatibility.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Maximum Results: Application Displays Correct Number of Restaurants
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the application displays the correct number of restaurants when displaying maximum results.

test_steps: Step 1: Launch the application on the iOS device and navigate to the restaurant list page

Step 3: Verify that the application displays exactly 100 restaurants on the list page
Step 4: Confirm that the application does not display more than 100 restaurants
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays exactly 100 restaurants when displaying maximum results, with no more or fewer restaurants.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Restaurant Profile: Application Displays Correct Information
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the application displays correct information for a specific restaurant profile.

test_steps: Step 1: Launch the application on the Android device and navigate to the restaurant list page
Step 2: Search for a specific restaurant by name or cuisine
Step 3: Verify that the application displays correct information for the selected restaurant, including distance and price details
Step 4: Confirm that the application does not display incorrect or missing information
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays correct information for a specific restaurant profile, including distance and price details.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Test Application Testability with Specific Environments
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that application test environments are built for different functions.

Step 2: Click 'Create Test Environment' button with name 'Restaurant Test'
Step 3: Enter a valid URL for the test environment, e.g., 'https://example.com'

phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application successfully creates a new test environment with the specified name and URL.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Test Invalid Test Environment Creation

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Test Maximum Test Environments Limit

Step 4: Confirm that no new test environment is created

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Test Integration with Other Features

Step 4: Confirm that selecting the test environment redirects to the correct URL

phase1_test_cases.test_title: GPS Connection Successful
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the application successfully connects to the GPS device.
preconditions: The application is installed and running
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main menu at /main
Step 2: Click 'Connect to GPS' button with no input
Step 3: Verify the GPS connection status indicator displays 'Connected'
Step 4: Confirm the application's GPS-related features are accessible
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application successfully connects to the GPS device, displaying a connected status and enabling GPS-related features.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: GPS Connection Failure
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the application handles GPS connection failure scenarios.

Step 2: Click 'Connect to GPS' button with invalid input (e.g., incorrect URL)
Step 3: Verify an error message displays indicating failed connection attempt
Step 4: Confirm the application's GPS-related features remain inaccessible
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application correctly handles GPS connection failure scenarios, displaying an error message and preventing access to GPS-related features.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: GPS Connection Edge Case: No GPS Device

Step 3: Verify an error message displays indicating no GPS device available
Step 4: Confirm the application's GPS-related features remain inaccessible
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application correctly handles scenarios where no GPS device is available, displaying an error message and preventing access to GPS-related features.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: GPS Connection Integration with Restaurant List
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the application integrates GPS connection with restaurant list display.

Step 2: Click 'Connect to GPS' button with valid input (e.g., correct URL)
Step 3: Verify the restaurant list displays with GPS-related information (e.g., distance, directions)
Step 4: Confirm the application's GPS-related features remain accessible and functional
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application successfully integrates GPS connection with restaurant list display, providing accurate GPS-related information and enabling GPS-related features.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User-friendly list view with results displayed correctly

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Mobile App Download Performance Test: Initial Load
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile app downloads and initializes within a reasonable time frame.
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile app on a device with a stable internet connection
Step 2: Measure the time it takes for the app to download and initialize (approx. 10 seconds)
Step 3: Verify that the app's UI elements are displayed correctly, including menu items and icons
Step 4: Check that the app's core features are functional, such as searching for restaurants or placing orders
Step 5: Measure the time it takes to perform a simple action, like searching for 'pizza' (approx. 2 seconds)
Step 6: Verify that the search results are displayed correctly and within the expected time frame
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile app downloads and initializes within 15 seconds, with a search result display time of less than 3 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Mobile App Download Performance Test: Subsequent Loads
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile app loads and initializes quickly after the initial download.
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile app on a device with a stable internet connection
Step 2: Measure the time it takes for the app to load and initialize (approx. 5 seconds)
Step 3: Verify that the app's UI elements are displayed correctly, including menu items and icons
Step 4: Check that the app's core features are functional, such as searching for restaurants or placing orders
Step 5: Measure the time it takes to perform a simple action, like searching for 'pizza' (approx. 1 second)
Step 6: Verify that the search results are displayed correctly and within the expected time frame
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile app loads and initializes within 8 seconds, with a search result display time of less than 2 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Mobile App Download Performance Test: Large Data Load
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile app can handle large amounts of data and load quickly.
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile app on a device with a stable internet connection
Step 2: Measure the time it takes for the app to load and initialize (approx. 10 seconds)
Step 3: Simulate a large data load by searching for a specific category (e.g., 'italian restaurants')
Step 4: Verify that the app's UI elements are displayed correctly, including menu items and icons
Step 5: Check that the app's core features are functional, such as filtering search results or viewing restaurant details
Step 6: Measure the time it takes to perform a simple action, like searching for 'pizza' (approx. 2 seconds)
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile app loads and initializes within 15 seconds, with a search result display time of less than 3 seconds for large data sets.

phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that an unauthorized user cannot access or download the mobile application.
preconditions: The application is installed on a device
test_steps: Step 1: Log in as an unauthorized user (e.g., fake email and password) and attempt to access the downloaded application
Step 2: Verify that the application prompts for login credentials or denies access due to lack of authentication
Step 3: Attempt to download the application again using a different device or account
Step 4: Verify that the application is not accessible or downloadable without proper authentication
Step 5: Confirm that the application's security features (e.g., encryption, secure storage) are enabled and functioning as expected
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application denies access or download attempts without proper authentication, with a specific error message (e.g., 'Invalid login credentials') and no sensitive data is exposed.

phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application's data is encrypted during download and transmission.
preconditions: The application is installed on a device
test_steps: Step 1: Enable Wi-Fi or cellular connection on the device to simulate network transmission
Step 2: Download the application while monitoring network traffic using a tool like Wireshark
Step 3: Verify that the downloaded data is encrypted (e.g., SSL/TLS) and transmitted securely over the network
Step 4: Attempt to intercept or modify the transmitted data without proper decryption keys
Step 5: Confirm that any attempts to access or manipulate the decrypted data result in errors or warnings
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application's data is transmitted securely and encrypted during download, with a specific encryption algorithm (e.g., AES-256) and no unauthorized access or modification is possible.

phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application does not allow malicious code injection during download or execution.
preconditions: The application is installed on a device
test_steps: Step 1: Enable developer mode and USB debugging on the device (if applicable)
Step 2: Use a tool like Frida or Cydia to inject custom code into the application
Step 3: Verify that the injected code is not executed or has no effect on the application's behavior
Step 4: Attempt to inject malicious code that attempts to access sensitive data or perform unauthorized actions
Step 5: Confirm that any attempts to execute malicious code result in errors, warnings, or termination of the application
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application does not allow malicious code injection during download or execution, with a specific error message (e.g., 'Invalid code signature') and no sensitive data is exposed.

phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the user can successfully download and install the mobile application, with a smooth initial experience.
preconditions: No prior knowledge of the restaurant/food service
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile app store (e.g., Apple App Store or Google Play) on the mobile device
Step 2: Search for the restaurant/food service's mobile application and select it from search results
Step 3: Click the 'Get' or 'Install' button to initiate the download process
Step 4: Wait for the installation to complete, then launch the app
Step 5: Verify that the app opens successfully with a clear and intuitive interface
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The user can successfully download and install the mobile application within 30 seconds, with no errors or issues.

phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the user can recover from errors during the download process, with clear and helpful error messages.
preconditions: Prior knowledge of the restaurant/food service
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate a network error (e.g., by disconnecting the mobile device from Wi-Fi) during the download process
Step 2: Observe the error message displayed to the user, ensuring it is clear and helpful
Step 3: Wait for the error to resolve, then retry the download process
Step 4: Verify that the app installs successfully after resolving the error
Step 5: Repeat steps 1-4 with different types of errors (e.g., server-side issues)
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The user can recover from download errors within 60 seconds, with clear and helpful error messages that provide actionable feedback.

phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the download process is consistent across iOS and Android devices.

Step 3: Download the app on both devices and verify that the download process completes successfully
Step 4: Launch the app on both devices and verify that the login screen appears with actual login credentials (e.g., 'username' and 'password')

test_data.expected_result: The download process completes successfully on both iOS and Android devices, with identical app icon, title, and description.

phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the app's layout and functionality are consistent across different screen sizes and resolutions.

test_steps: Step 1: Launch the app on a device with a small screen size (e.g., iPhone SE) and verify that all UI elements are displayed correctly
Step 2: Launch the app on a device with a medium screen size (e.g., iPhone 12) and verify that all UI elements are displayed correctly
Step 3: Launch the app on a device with a large screen size (e.g., iPad Pro) and verify that all UI elements are displayed correctly
Step 4: Verify that the app's layout and functionality remain consistent across all devices, including scrolling and zooming behaviors

test_data.expected_result: The app's layout and functionality are consistent across all devices with varying screen sizes and resolutions.

phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the app's GPS and network type functionality are consistent across different devices and network types.

test_steps: Step 1: Launch the app on a device with a strong GPS signal (e.g., iPhone 12) and verify that location-based features work correctly
Step 2: Launch the app on a device with a weak GPS signal (e.g., older Android device) and verify that location-based features work correctly
Step 3: Test the app's functionality on different network types, including WiFi, 4G, and 5G
Step 4: Verify that the app's GPS and network type functionality remain consistent across all devices and network types
Step 5: Test specific features like order tracking and delivery updates to ensure they work consistently across different devices and network types
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app's GPS and network type functionality are consistent across all devices with varying GPS capabilities and network types.

test_data.expected_result: The app's GPS and network type functionality are consistent across all devices with varying GPS capabilities and network types.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: API Test: Download Mobile Application - Successful Request
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that a successful request to download the mobile application returns the correct response.
preconditions: The mobile application is available for download
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/download-mobile-app with header Accept: application/octet-stream
Step 2: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 3: Verify response body contains the mobile application binary (exact file size: 10.5 MB)
Step 4: Verify response headers include 'Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=mobile-app.apk'
Step 5: Verify response headers include 'Content-Type: application/octet-stream'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application binary is successfully downloaded and the file size matches the expected value (10.5 MB).

phase2_test_cases.test_title: API Test: Download Mobile Application - Invalid Request
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that an invalid request to download the mobile application returns an error response.
preconditions: The mobile application is available for download
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/download-mobile-app with header Accept: text/plain
Step 2: Verify HTTP 400 status code returned
Step 3: Verify response body contains an error message indicating the incorrect Accept header
Step 4: Verify response headers include 'Content-Type: application/json'
Step 5: Verify response headers do not include 'Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=mobile-app.apk'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns an error response with a HTTP 400 status code and an error message indicating the incorrect Accept header.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: API Test: Download Mobile Application - Rate Limiting
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the API enforces rate limiting for downloading the mobile application.
preconditions: The mobile application is available for download
test_steps: Step 1: Send multiple GET requests to /api/download-mobile-app with header Accept: application/octet-stream within a short time frame (less than 2 seconds)
Step 2: Verify HTTP 429 status code returned after the second request
Step 3: Verify response body contains an error message indicating rate limiting exceeded
Step 4: Verify response headers include 'Content-Type: application/json'
Step 5: Verify response headers do not include 'Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=mobile-app.apk'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API enforces rate limiting and returns an error response with a HTTP 429 status code after the second request within the short time frame.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: CRUD Operations Validation: Restaurant Menu Items
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete) on restaurant menu items
preconditions: User has logged in and is on the main menu page
test_steps: Step 1: Create a new menu item with name 'New Item' and price $10.99; verify it appears in the list of menu items
Step 2: Read the details of the newly created menu item; verify the name and price match the input values
Step 3: Update the price of the menu item to $11.99; verify the change is reflected in the list of menu items
Step 4: Delete the menu item; verify it no longer appears in the list of menu items
Step 5: Attempt to create a new menu item with an invalid name (e.g., ''); verify an error message is displayed
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All CRUD operations are validated correctly, with no errors or inconsistencies in the menu item data (minimum 30 characters)

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Mandatory vs Optional Field Validation: Customer Information
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the mandatory and optional fields for customer information
preconditions: User has started filling out the customer information form
test_steps: Step 1: Attempt to submit the form without entering a name; verify an error message is displayed indicating 'Name' is required
Step 2: Enter a valid name and attempt to submit the form without entering an email address; verify the form is submitted successfully

Step 3: Enter both a valid name and email address, then attempt to submit the form with an invalid phone number (e.g., '123-4567'); verify an error message is displayed indicating 'Phone Number' is invalid
Step 4: Enter all required fields (name, email, phone number) and submit the form; verify the customer information is saved correctly
Step 5: Attempt to edit a previously submitted customer profile with incomplete or invalid data; verify an error message is displayed indicating 'Incomplete/Invalid Data'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All mandatory and optional fields are validated correctly, with no errors or inconsistencies in the customer information data (minimum 30 characters)

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Transaction Integrity: Order Placement and Payment
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the transaction integrity of order placement and payment
preconditions: User has added items to their cart and is on the checkout page
test_steps: Step 1: Place an order with a total value of $50.00; verify the order is successfully placed and the payment method is set to 'Credit Card'
Step 2: Verify the order details, including items, subtotal, tax, and total, are accurate
Step 3: Attempt to place an order with an invalid credit card number (e.g., '1234-5678-9012-3456'); verify an error message is displayed indicating 'Invalid Credit Card'
Step 4: Place an order with a valid credit card and verify the payment processing is successful
Step 5: Verify the order status is updated to 'Paid' or 'Processing'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All transaction integrity checks are validated correctly, with no errors or inconsistencies in the order placement and payment data (minimum 30 characters)

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling: App Crash Recovery
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the app recovers from a network interruption and crashes, preserving user data.
test_steps: Step 1: Start the app and log in to an existing account
Step 2: Simulate a network interruption by disabling Wi-Fi or mobile data
Step 3: Observe the app's behavior and wait for it to crash
Step 4: Re-enable the network connection and restart the app
Step 5: Verify that the app recovers and displays the previous user data without losing any information
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app recovers from the crash, displaying the previous account name 'JohnDoe123' with no data loss.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: GPS Signal Loss Behavior: App Crash Recovery
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the app recovers from a GPS signal loss and crashes, preserving user data.
test_steps: Step 1: Start the app and log in to an existing account
Step 2: Simulate a GPS signal loss by disabling location services
Step 3: Observe the app's behavior and wait for it to crash
Step 4: Re-enable location services and restart the app
Step 5: Verify that the app recovers and displays the previous user data without losing any information
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app recovers from the crash, displaying the previous account name 'JaneDoe456' with no data loss.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Session Timeout Handling: Data Persistence Across Crashes
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the app persists user data across crashes and session timeouts.
test_steps: Step 1: Start the app and log in to an existing account
Step 2: Simulate a session timeout by closing the app or locking the screen
Step 3: Observe the app's behavior and wait for it to crash
Step 4: Re-start the app and verify that user data is preserved
Step 5: Verify that the app displays the previous account information without any loss
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app persists user data across crashes and session timeouts, displaying the previous account name 'BobSmith789' with no data loss.
